Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Google releases Gemma 4 open models 77 upvotes, 7 comments
  2. Lemonade by AMD: a fast and open source local LLM server using GPU and NPU 245 upvotes, 60 comments
  3. Renewables reached nearly 50% of global electricity capacity last year 29 upvotes, 4 comments
  4. Email obfuscation: What works in 2026? 272 upvotes, 83 comments
  5. Quantum computing bombshells that are not April Fools 240 upvotes, 78 comments
  6. Mercor says it was hit by cyberattack tied to compromise LiteLLM 115 upvotes, 34 comments
  7. Snow melt-off in American west stuns scientists 8 upvotes, 0 comments
  8. On the trail of ancient art, deep in the Sahara 12 upvotes, 1 comments
  9. LinkedIn Is Illegally Searching Your Computer 900 upvotes, 402 comments
  10. Emacs-libgterm: Terminal emulator for Emacs using libghostty-vt 50 upvotes, 15 comments
  11. Qwen3.6-Plus: Towards Real World Agents 161 upvotes, 65 comments
  12. Steam on Linux Use Skyrocketed Above 5% in March 621 upvotes, 291 comments
  13. Inside Nepal's Fake Rescue Racket 151 upvotes, 64 comments
  14. Sweden goes back to basics, swapping screens for books in the classroom 488 upvotes, 241 comments
  15. Built a cheap DIY fan controller because my motherboard never had working PWM 61 upvotes, 23 comments
  16. Significant Raise of Reports 163 upvotes, 77 comments
  17. Gone (Almost) Phishin' 127 upvotes, 59 comments
  18. Enabling Codex to Analyze Two Decades of Hacker News Data 51 upvotes, 21 comments
  19. Delve allegedly forked an open-source tool and sold it as its own 75 upvotes, 37 comments
  20. Artemis computer running two instances of MS outlook; they can't figure out why 72 upvotes, 36 comments
  21. IBM Announces Strategic Collaboration with Arm 212 upvotes, 129 comments
  22. Bringing Clojure programming to Enterprise (2021) 139 upvotes, 80 comments
  23. An Example of Statistical Investigation of the Text Eugene Onegin – Markov, 1913 [pdf] 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  24. Artemis II will use laser beams to live-stream 4K moon footage at 260 Mbps 66 upvotes, 34 comments
  25. 'Backrooms' and the Rise of the Institutional Gothic 52 upvotes, 26 comments
  26. EmDash – A spiritual successor to WordPress that solves plugin security 643 upvotes, 480 comments
  27. Show HN: I built a DNS resolver from scratch in Rust – no DNS libraries 61 upvotes, 37 comments
  28. The SpaceX IPO: retail investor notes 66 upvotes, 43 comments
  29. Artemis II Launch Day Updates 1025 upvotes, 899 comments
  30. Telli (YC F24) is hiring engineers, designers, and more (on-site, Berlin) 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 12:30am, 3 April 2026. Source here.
